workflows-reference.purpose: Provide Vertical Slice plus CUPID/YAGNI/KISS workflows for Compass tasks.

documentation-context.rule: Read docs/architecture/vertical-slice-architecture.md and docs/architecture/feature-slice-structure.md before design or implementation.
Read docs/foundation/cupid-yagni-principles.md before creating abstractions or shared code.
Read relevant docs/modules notes for the touched feature area.

task-memory-gate.rule: For long or risky work with at least two concrete implementation slices after alignment or design, load Compass references/task-memory.xml.
Implementation may not start until the task memory outcome is reported as created, resumed, or not-required.
When task memory is required, docs/.tasks/<task>/goal.md, diagram.md, and memories.md must exist before the first implementation edit or command.
Missing docs/_templates task memory files are a documentation gap, not permission to skip this gate.

workflow.description: Add one feature-local behavior through a vertical slice.

enforcement.rule: Run phases sequentially: slice discovery, slice design, implementation.
Execute exactly one behavior slice per workflow.
Do not create global layers or shared abstractions before current callers prove the need.
Design should be lightweight enough to keep implementation local.
Implementation must stop after each verified slice checkpoint.
Before implementation starts, satisfy the task-memory-gate for any long or risky multi-slice feature.

phase.name: slice-discovery
phase.purpose: Name the user-visible command, query, or workflow and decide the smallest useful slice.

actions.action: Read the vertical slice architecture, feature-slice structure, CUPID/YAGNI, and incremental test docs.
Restate the behavior as a command, query, endpoint, or workflow step.
Identify the feature area and proposed slice folder.
Split unrelated behaviors into pending slices.
State non-goals and what will stay local for now.

phase-output.non-goals: Behaviors not included in this slice.

phase.name: slice-design
phase.purpose: Design only the local slice shape and closest useful tests.

actions.action: Choose slice files from docs/architecture/feature-slice-structure.md.
Define request or command or query input, handler responsibility, result or response, local persistence needs, and emitted events if any.
Apply CUPID: composable, single-purpose, predictable, idiomatic, and domain-based names.
Apply YAGNI: reject abstractions without current callers.
Create an incremental test matrix: local policy, handler, persistence, endpoint, or workflow tests as needed.

phase-output.slice-structure: Files and ownership inside the slice.
phase-output.test-matrix: Closest useful tests from common path to failure paths.

phase.name: implementation
phase.purpose: Implement the approved vertical slice through small checkpoints.

actions.action: Before the first implementation edit or command, report the task memory outcome: created, resumed, or not-required.
Keep code mostly inside the selected slice folder.
Implement the closest behavior first, then adapters or persistence only when needed.
Run slice-level verification first.
Broaden verification only when shared code, platform, public contracts, or persistence changed.
Stop and ask before implementing the next slice.

phase-output.shared-changes: Shared code changed and why, or none.

workflow.evidence: Approved slice design, local behavior tests, shared-code justification if any, and docs update when boundaries change.

workflow.description: Change an existing feature slice by controlling the delta from protected slice behavior.

enforcement.rule: Run phases sequentially: baseline-discovery, delta-design, implementation-checkpoints, and rollout-cleanup when rollout guards or compatibility paths are introduced.
Classify as architecture_change first when the update changes slice boundaries, shared platform ownership, or cross-slice contracts.
Before implementation starts, satisfy the task-memory-gate for any long, risky, or multi-slice update.
Keep changes local to the owning slice unless current callers prove a shared extraction is needed.
High-risk updates must define rollback, feature-flag, dark-launch, compatibility, or cleanup evidence before implementation.

phase.name: baseline-discovery
phase.purpose: Protect current slice behavior before deciding what may change.

actions.action: Find the owning feature slice, similar behavior, slice-local tests, shared callers, docs, commands, and composition points.
State current behavior, cross-slice contracts, shared dependencies, configuration, and user-visible expectations that must remain stable.
Classify update risk as minor, medium, or high based on blast radius, reversibility, data impact, user visibility, and slice-boundary impact.
Record ambiguity before designing the delta.

phase-output.baseline: Owning slice, behavior, tests, shared callers, docs, and verification commands observed.
phase-output.preserve-list: Behavior, cross-slice contracts, shared callers, and non-goals that must not drift.
phase-output.risk-level: minor, medium, or high with reason.

phase.name: delta-design
phase.purpose: Define the exact slice behavior change and regression evidence before editing.

actions.action: State old behavior, new behavior, unchanged behavior, and intentionally removed behavior if any.
Define slice-local regression tests for preserved behavior and changed-behavior tests in the existing style.
Define the smallest owning slice files to change and justify any shared-code change with current caller evidence.
Decide whether the update needs a feature flag, dark launch, compatibility path, rollback plan, monitoring, docs update, or decision record.
Define the implementation slices and verification command for each slice.

phase-output.delta: Approved behavior change and unchanged behavior.
phase-output.tests: Slice-local regression and changed-behavior test plan.
phase-output.shared-change: Shared extraction or shared edit justification, or none.
phase-output.rollout-risk: Flag, rollback, monitoring, compatibility, cleanup, or not needed.
phase-output.docs-impact: Docs or decisions to update, or none.

phase.name: implementation-checkpoints
phase.purpose: Apply one approved delta slice while keeping the change local first.

actions.action: Before the first implementation edit or command, report the task memory outcome: created, resumed, or not-required.
Add or update the approved slice-local tests for the current slice when practical.
Change code locally inside the owning slice first and avoid introducing competing shared patterns.
Run the current slice verification command, then broader tests only if boundaries or shared code changed.
Stop after each verified slice and ask before continuing.

phase-output.shared-changes: Shared code changed and why, or none.

phase.name: rollout-cleanup
phase.purpose: Close temporary risk controls introduced for the update.

actions.action: Record feature flag, dark launch, compatibility path, monitoring, rollback, or cleanup status when any were introduced.
Remove stale flags or inactive compatibility paths after the update is fully accepted.
Update docs, decisions, public contracts, or runbooks when behavior or rollout expectations changed.
Run final verification for the touched scope.

phase-output.rollout-state: Flag, rollout, compatibility, rollback, or cleanup state.

workflow.evidence: Approved baseline, approved delta design, slice-local regression coverage for preserved behavior, changed-behavior verification, shared-change justification, slice checkpoints, and rollout or cleanup evidence when needed.

steps.step: Reproduce the bug in the owning slice.
Add a focused regression test at the closest useful level.
Run slice tests and broaden only when shared code changed.

workflow.evidence: Regression test, local fix, and targeted verification.

steps.step: Identify whether the change is a slice boundary, shared platform, or cross-slice contract change.
Compare local duplication, shared extraction, and platform abstraction.
Document why YAGNI no longer applies if extracting shared code.
Implement in one affected slice or shared area at a time.
Run affected slice tests and broader boundary tests.

workflow.evidence: Decision record, caller evidence, affected slice tests, and boundary verification.
